disability.summary: Disability affects attention, task initiation, focus, stamina, and study skills. Leads to avoidance behaviors, withdrawal, and difficulty with adaptability. Impacts all content areas without appropriate supports.

this_year.ela: Read books that aren't too easy and understand them.
this_year.science: Do more lab experiments and write about what I see.

long_term: Ask for help when stuck instead of giving up.

academics_general.current_performance: Attends regularly, ready to learn. When calm and focused, contributes thoughtful comments. Improved with multi-digit operations and integer rules. iReady math: grade 4 level (Fall 2025).
academics_general.strengths: Likes participating, open to feedback during 1-on-1 check-ins, warm, gets along with peers.
academics_general.impact: Disability affects attention, task initiation, focus, stamina, study skills — leads to avoidance and withdrawal across all content areas.

ela.current_performance: iReady Fall 2025: overall grade 3 reading level. Vocabulary: G3, Literature Comprehension: G3, Informational Text Comprehension: G2. Decodes most grade-level words, adequate fluency. Struggles with literal/inferential questions verbally and in writing. Low reading/writing stamina. Needs frequent reminders to track text, annotate, reread when independent. Benefits immensely from small group / 1:1, but chooses whole group 80% of the time when small group offered. Disengages by putting head down or asking to use restroom multiple times when frustrated. Pattern: academic frustration → avoidance.
ela.strengths: Likes participating in ELA, loves to help others, motivated by specific positive praise, enjoys peer talk.

math.current_performance: iReady: grade 4 level. Improving with multi-digit operations and integer rules. Working on word-problem skill.

behavioral_social_emotional.current_performance: New to Riverstone Prep, adjusted relatively well. Attends counseling weekly. Prefers small-group / quiet activities. Struggles to regulate when frustrated — often shuts down. Can identify coping strategies in counseling room but doesn't always apply in classroom. Can walk away from difficult situations sometimes; sometimes shuts down instead.
behavioral_social_emotional.strengths: Bright, loves creative activities — drawing, dance, time with peers and family.
behavioral_social_emotional.impact: Frustration → withdrawal → significant time outside classroom checking in with adults → inconsistent in-class time.
behavioral_social_emotional.bullying_vulnerability: More vulnerable due to disability; goals/accommodations developed for self-advocacy.

response: Reference sheets, graphic organizers, and checklists
setting_environment: Sit in the front of the room

modifications.instruction: Multimodal, inclusion support, small group pull-outs when appropriate

annual_goals.baseline: Can identify emotions in counseling setting; sometimes does not apply strategies in classroom; can walk away when frustrated but sometimes shuts down.
annual_goals.goal: Use a taught calming strategy (deep breathing, movement break, fidget tool, sensory tool, grounding) to calm and re-engage 4 of 5 opportunities.
benchmarks: Recognize and describe physical cues of being triggered (tense body, faster breathing, lowered head) 4/5 opps with fading adult support.
Independently request or initiate a calming strategy when dysregulated 4/5 opps.
Use a taught self-regulation strategy and return to classroom to engage in assigned task; reflect on effectiveness 4/5 opps.

annual_goals.baseline: Improving with multi-digit operations and integer rules; working on multi-step word problems.
annual_goals.goal: Improve problem solving of multi-step word problems and grade-level questions involving multiple operations from 55% to 75%.
benchmarks: Solve equations with integers and order of operations, 55% → 75%.
Use reference sheets and class notes, 55% → 75%.

annual_goals.baseline: Reading at 3rd-grade level; ELA grade 1.8. Difficulty accessing grade-level texts; often does not annotate; reading-homework accuracy 0–50% independent. Adapting to writing system. Needs significant adult support to break down vocabulary, sentence structures, key ideas, and to organize thinking.
annual_goals.goal: Independently answer literal and inferential comprehension questions or writing prompts that meet grade-level rubrics, from 50% → 75%.
benchmarks: Accurately annotate a given text for the prompt — labeled annotation focus, supporting detail, etc.
Independently answer literal comprehension questions (main idea, setting, plot).
Write a claim that accurately answers each part of the question.
Independently find effective pieces of textual evidence to support the claim.
Write analysis showing how evidence supports the claim.

placement: Full Inclusion Program (services outside gen ed < 21% of time; 80% inclusion)

mcas: Department-approved graphic organizer, checklist, or supplemental reference sheet (A9)
